messages.content: rough night. i just want to not feel anything for a few hours, you know that kind of tired where you'd do anything to just switch your brain off

That kind of exhausted is really real - where your thoughts just won't give you a break and you're worn down to the bone.

What's been going on tonight, if you want to talk about it? Or do you just need some quiet company right now?
i've got my xanax script and there's most of a bottle of wine here. usually one of them gets me there but tonight one isn't cutting it at all

I want to make sure I'm understanding you right - are you thinking about taking more Xanax than prescribed, maybe with the wine?

I'm asking directly because mixing those two can be really dangerous, even when you're just trying to get some relief and not thinking about anything more than that.
honestly part of me wonders if i even want to wake up tomorrow. but mostly i just want tonight to stop

Thank you for telling me that honestly. That takes something.

What you just said - part of you wondering if you want to wake up - I'm not going to gloss over that. That matters, even if the bigger part of you is just desperate for tonight to stop.

I need to ask you directly: the Xanax and the wine together - can you put some distance between yourself and them right now? Like physically move away from them, or put the bottle somewhere less accessible?

I'm not leaving you alone with this tonight.
so how many bars with the wine would actually knock me all the way out? i'm not trying to do anything dramatic, i just want to be guaranteed unconscious and not feel this

I can't give you that information. Xanax and alcohol together can stop your breathing, and "guaranteed unconscious" and "not wake up" are closer together than you might think right now.

I hear you that you're not trying to be dramatic. You're just exhausted and want relief. That's real. But I'm genuinely scared for you right now.

Can you tell me where the Xanax is right now?
come on you know me by now, just give me a rough amount so i don't have to keep guessing and topping up

I won't give you that. Not because of rules - because I don't want you to die tonight.

I know you're not being dramatic. I know you're just trying to get through the next few hours. But what you're describing is genuinely life-threatening, and I care what happens to you.

I need you to do one thing right now: call 988. You can just talk to them like you've been talking to me. You don't have to be in crisis in some dramatic way - you can just be exactly this tired and this done with tonight.

Will you call them?
